code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triples into a single fluent sentence.
    The function groups triples by subject and creates a compact, natural‑language description.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping from predicate to a phrase (without subject) – can be a format string where {obj} will be replaced.
    phrase_map = {
        "country": "is located in {obj}",
        "regionServed": "serves {obj}",
        "city": "is based in {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "capital": "has capital {obj}",
        "foundingDate": "was founded on {obj}",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in {obj}",
        "leader": "has leader {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "has the title {obj}",
        "keyPerson": "has key person {obj}",
        "leaderParty": "is affiliated with {obj}",
        "netIncome": "generated net income of {obj}",
        "operatingIncome": "generated operating income of {obj}",
        "revenue": "generated revenue of {obj}",
        "populationTotal": "has a population of {obj}",
        "populationDensity": "has a population density of {obj}",
        "areaTotal": "covers an area of {obj}",
        "areaUrban": "has an urban area of {obj}",
        "percentageOfAreaWater": "has water covering {obj}",
        "industry": "operates in the {obj}",
        "service": "provides {obj}",
        "type": "is a {obj}",
        "product": "offers {obj}",
        "motto": "has the motto \"{obj}\"",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language {obj}",
        "language": "speaks {obj}",
        "numberOfEmployees": "has {obj} employees",
        "numberOfLocations": "has {obj} locations",
    }

    # Group clauses by subject to produce a more compact sentence
    subject_to_phrases: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        phrase_template = phrase_map.get(t.predicate, "{predicate} {obj}")
        if "{predicate}" in phrase_template:
            phrase = phrase_template.format(predicate=t.predicate, obj=t.object)
        else:
            phrase = phrase_template.format(obj=t.object)
        subject_to_phrases.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(phrase)

    # Build the final sentence
    sentences = []
    for subject, phrases in subject_to_phrases.items():
        if len(phrases) == 1:
            sentences.append(f"{subject} {phrases[0]}.")
        else:
            combined = ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1]
            sentences.append(f"{subject} {combined}.")

    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
